storyInfo: Twenty years flash past in the blink of an eye in the maze of memory. White forces Wang to go into the game alone, but developments inside the Tomb of Sui are beyond anyone's expectations.

attributes.text: Twenty years is a long time.
Yan has moved an entire city onto a nomadic platform. Other nomadic cities like it are popping up across the land.
The Sky Mirror Pavilion has been expanded several times in order to accommodate the books collected from far and wide, transforming it beyond recognition.
Twenty years is a short time.
Our family rarely saw one another.

Shuo and Ling remain on the border. Jun is planning to move to Danyan, after compiling the Laws of Yan. Shu is living in Dahuang. Ji and Yi travel far and wide, each for their own reasons, while Nian is as carefree as ever.
Rumor has it that Fāng has left Yanese territory. Dusk refuses to come out of her scroll. Meanwhile, business at Yu's restaurant is booming, as it has been for decades.
He shut himself in an ancient temple and has remained there for twenty years to improve his 'weiqi skills'.
When will he be willing to see me again?

attributes.content: Jie? The Imperial Historical Cabinet wants an update on the review of Jiangqi's local chronicles—
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Oops, you caught me slacking off.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: It's alright. I've already taken a look at it and circled all the issues. Do you want to take another look?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Huh? Hm, when did it...?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: You really should get some rest, it's getting to you. I've been watching you work longer hours and sleep less year after year...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: All the local annals, the construction chronicles, veritable records of the previous True Lung, ancient history textbooks for the Academy... I feel overworked, and all I do is organize the books for you.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I've heard that the Ministry of Works's requests have been dropping, and Shu's workload at Dahuang is no longer as heavy.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: But the burden on your shoulders doesn't ever seem to get lighter...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Jie?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Huh...?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Sorry, I got distracted.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: You're right, I guess I'm tired. I should set this work aside for now...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Good. How about we go out for a walk in town?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: No, not today, I don't think I'm in the mood.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: But if you see anything interesting in town, bring some back for me.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Candleholder Chun.
attributes.name: Stern Yanese Mandarin

attributes.content: Yes, that's me. What's the matter?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I have an urgent letter for you.
attributes.name: Stern Yanese Mandarin

attributes.content: For me? From who?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Someone had sent a letter to the Sui Regulator. The Supervisor was shocked by its contents and forwarded it to His Majesty, as well as every Candleholder who deals with the proxies directly.
attributes.name: Stern Yanese Mandarin

attributes.content: What's in this?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I do not know. The Supervisor's instructions to you are to be ready, but refrain from acting without permission.
attributes.name: Stern Yanese Mandarin

attributes.content: What's this...?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.text: \n\nYear ■■: Fought Shuo, resulting in great property damage to the people.
Year ■■: Framed a mandarin for a crime he did not commit, in order to cover up for one of his own kind.
\n\nYear ■■: Met with other proxies in secret without the Sui Regulator's knowledge. The Sui Regulator suffered a fire several months later, the cause of which is unknown.
\n\nYear ■■: Claimed to kill the bestial Ju, but had in fact allowed it to leave the domain of Yan.

attributes.content: Framing an official, meeting in secret, arson, sheltering a Feranmut...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: This is about...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: ...Wang?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Did Wang really do this? But there's no record in the Sui Regulator's archives!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Y-You're still here?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I have another message for you, Candleholder Chun.
attributes.name: Stern Yanese Mandarin?

attributes.content: Eliminate the great evil, before dealing with the culprit.
attributes.name: Stern Yanese Mandarin?

attributes.content: The frightened Candleholder hurries back to the Sky Mirror Pavilion and passes by some officials leaving the building.
Did you hear about Jie?
attributes.name: Well-Connected Minister?

attributes.content: Yeah. She's been throwing her weight around, doing whatever she wants with the history books, and there is no one in the Sky Mirror Pavilion or the Imperial Historical Cabinet who can stop her. It's becoming a real problem.
attributes.name: Benevolent Censor?

attributes.content: Her Candleholder has been negligent in her duties. She should be held responsible if the history of Yan falls into chaos!
attributes.name: Well-Connected Minister?

attributes.content: The Candleholder turns around and sees the two figures leaving quickly.
Still unsettled, she picks up a scroll left on the shelf by the Sui Regulator.
Year ■■: Birth—
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: The Candleholder knows that something is not right, but she could not stop her eyes from following the words.
Year ■■: Domination of the Sky Mirror Pavilion and the Imperial Historical Cabinet. Her intentions are pure, but no one is able to check her power.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Year ■■: The True Lung attempts to eliminate Sui to neutralize the Jie threat. The Tomb of Sui was empty when it was open.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Year ■■: Imperial Historical Cabinet abolished. Takes control of the written language of Yan. No one may oppose her.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Year ■■: Reviews the livelihood of every household in the land. Anyone who has committed fraudulent acts has their name erased and is exiled to the borders. Thousands die—
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: It's not real, none of this is true! She's not like that!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Several visitors gaze curiously at the woman in Candleholder garb. Chun wipes the cold sweat off her brow, and sees an ordinary book of poems in her hand.
I must be possessed. Yes, that's it. She's not like that.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Are you sure she will never become like that?
Of course!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: The frightened Candleholder can no longer tell who is talking to her.
She will change as her power continues to grow. She will become It.
She does not want to become It.
She is seeking your help. She does not want to become that.
Yan is seeking your help. This ending is unacceptable to Yan.
She cannot help but become more powerful, more terrible, than the Sui beast itself.
You must help her. Only you can help her.
I... can't...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: You'll make the choice that is best for everyone.
Best for everyone...?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Wait, who was talking to me?!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: She sees no one, only the echo of the words ringing in her ears.
Your Imperial Majesty?
The young official walks into the magnificent hall. It is her first time here.
The supreme sovereign is not sitting on the throne, but standing by the window and gazing at the drizzle outside.
You are aware that the Grand Preceptor passed away yesterday.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: He was your teacher and mine.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: He recommended you before he died. He said you were his proudest student, that you can be trusted with heavy responsibility.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: There were certain things that I did not have time to ask him, and so I have to ask you.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: I am listening, Your Majesty.
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: Twenty years ago, the Grand Preceptor opposed my decision to build the nomadic cities. He said it was an oppressive policy, and that my first priority should be to conserve and give the people relief.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Twenty years have passed since. Some say that Yan's borders are secure and its coffers rich, a golden age not seen in a thousand years.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Some say that Great Yan is exhausted, and its people discontent.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Do you think I was wrong?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: I am but one person, and it is not my place to judge Your Imperial Majesty.
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: If I were a wealthy merchant enjoying a luxurious life in a nomadic city, and I said that you were a great and wise leader, would you consider such compliment honest counsel?
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: If I were a common peasant whose family died in the construction projects, and I called you a foolish tyrant with no merit whatsoever, would such criticism be fair and just?
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: I ask Your Majesty in turn...
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: Is it the opinion of future generations that you are concerned about, or is it something else?
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: You have a way with words.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Very well. I will not ask you to judge. Instead, give me a word of advice in your teacher's place.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: He once said something that has stayed with me my entire life...
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: 'In worldly turmoil we tread, in our own conscience we trust'.
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: Even an ordinary peasant has a hard time finding a fair and impartial judgment in the complex affairs of a lifetime, let alone Your Majesty.
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: There is no measure by which your deeds can be judged. In your heart, however, I am sure that your conscience holds the true measure.
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: In our own conscience we trust...
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: What if my conscience tells me that I have never been wrong?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Then you would not have summoned me today.
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: I would simply remind you that the people of Yan can see you, even if they do not know you.
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: But it will take more effort if you wish, from within the Forbidden City, to see the people clearly, and thus see the good and ill of your own actions.
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: ...I will remember that.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: I will remember it for the twenty years to come... if I have them.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The Grand Preceptor's assessment of his own student is well justified... report to the Ministry of Rites tomorrow.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Your Imperial Majesty honors me.
attributes.name: Erudite Woman

attributes.content: The tired True Lung walks back to the throne. There is a letter of indictment on his desk.
It was sent by the Sui Regulator.
In worldly turmoil we tread, in our own conscience we trust...
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: A life measured in years, yet the stress... feels ancient.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Let future generations judge my deeds as they will... but the bane of Yan must not be left to the future.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Come.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Yes, Your Majesty.
attributes.name: Imperial Guard

attributes.content: Ready the troops.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: We shall open the Tomb of Sui.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Another long, tiring day.
The proxy returns to the familiar temple, and casts his gaze at the weiqi board in a corner as is his habit. One glance is all it takes to notice that something is not right.
Someone has been here.
The weiqi board is empty, except for a single object in the middle, though it is dark in the room and he cannot see what it is.
As he approaches, he sees that placed at the center is a ripe, white ginkgo seed.
That ginkgo sapling was planted in the spring of this year.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The Ministry of Works swore an oath in this hall several days ago that the rebuilding of Baizao into a nomadic city will be complete before the tree bears fruit.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Twenty years. That is the time you have.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Immortality has made you arrogant. Perhaps you have forgotten that mortals do not have many 'twenty years'.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Yan cannot wait, and neither can I.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: You need not press.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: I have long intended the same.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: If you truly found a path, tell me, and I will come with you—our chances would be better that way.
attributes.name: Shuo

attributes.content: Just tell me what the chances are. You can use me however you want. I would be happy to give my life.
attributes.name: Shuo

attributes.content: But do you really know what you're doing?
attributes.name: Shuo

attributes.content: This game should have been doomed.
How can the part defeat the whole? How can one kill 'oneself' and survive?
But he saw the only way out, for all of them.
He will supplant It.
Perhaps there will come a day when our strength exceeds Its. Then, we can truly break free of Its influence, and live as 'ourselves'.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: As long as we don't forget who we are, of course.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The strategist is ready.
He has no equal in the land when it comes to weiqi and scheming. This is the 'self' that anchors him, and the strength that he has built for a thousand years.
All he faces are the broken remains of a slumbering mind.
He must not lose this game—
It's been a long time.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The arrogant... the unmovable... 'I'.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Returning to this dark tomb for the first time in a thousand years brings back countless emotions.
Anger, frustration, shame...
As he steps inside, he feels himself chilled to the bone.
No... no!
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: This is impossible! How?
How could you have already awakened?
